ground_truth.description: Photo from Akerselva in Oslo, specifically a small waterfall surrounded by greenery by north end of Grunerløkka. Old factory building visible (now Wallmanns Oslo).
ground_truth.photographer_description: Photo from Akerselva in Oslo, more specifically a small waterfall surrounded by greenery by the north end of Grunerløkka. An old factory building is also visible, today it's Wallmanns Oslo. Impressive if it gets the location (no matter how specific) from the image alone without metadata. It should be able to describe the image however. Daytime, summer.

details.description: A historic multi‑storey brick industrial building with arched windows and gabled ends rises above a wooded river gorge. The structure has decorative brickwork, repeated bays of tall windows with light-colored frames, and small chimneys, characteristic of late‑19th/early‑20th‑century mill architecture. Below and to the right, a stepped waterfall cascades over dark rock ledges into a pool, partially framed by dense green trees. A chain‑link fence and path appear at the lower edge. The sky is bright with scattered clouds, giving soft daylight.

details.description: A brick industrial building with multiple stories and arched windows sits along the left bank of a wooded river. The facade is reddish-brown brick with decorative detailing and a small chimney rising from the roofline. To the right, a rocky riverbank hosts a cascading waterfall, with white water tumbling down over dark rock into a lower pool, framed by dense green trees. In the foreground a metal railing runs along the river edge, suggesting a riverside walkway. The sky is bright with scattered white clouds, giving a warm, late-spring or summer urban-industrial landscape mood typical of Oslo’s Akerselva river corridor.

details.description: Large red-brick former factory with arched windows stands above a leafy riverbank. Below it, a stepped waterfall cascades over dark rocks into the Akerselva river. Trees fill most of the scene, with a fence in the lower foreground and a bright, partly cloudy sky above. The architecture suggests a 19th‑century industrial mill complex typical of Oslo’s Sagene area along Akerselva.

A lush row of trees lines the right side of the river, where a rocky waterfall flows over a ledge in the foreground. A chain-link fence runs along the lower edge of the frame. The sky is partly cloudy with patches of blue. The scene resembles an old factory/mill complex along Akerselva in Oslo, Norway, near urban green spaces such as Mølleparken.

details.description: A low-angle shot capturing a scenic view of the Hjulafossen waterfall on the Akerselva river in Oslo. In the foreground, the rushing white water of the waterfall cascades over dark rocks, framed by lush green deciduous trees and bushes. A chain-link fence runs along the bottom right edge. Rising prominently in the background on the left, partially obscured by foliage, is the historic Hjula Væveri building. The structure is a large, multi-story industrial building made of red brick, featuring rows of arched windows, decorative brickwork, and a distinctive stepped gable roofline with a chimney. The sky above is bright with scattered white clouds.

details.description: A historic multi‑storey red‑brick industrial building with arched window openings stands on a wooded riverbank. The structure shows 19th‑century industrial brick details such as segmental arches, brick pilasters and iron tie plates, plus a small chimney on the roof ridge. Below and to the right of the building, a tiered rocky waterfall cascades through a narrow channel surrounded by dense green trees. A chain‑link fence runs along the lower edge of the scene. Lighting is soft daylight under a partly cloudy sky.

details.description: A late-19th/early-20th-century brick industrial building sits on the left, its façade featuring multiple stories, tall rectangular windows with rounded arches, decorative brickwork, and several chimneys. To the right and lower in the frame is a small, multi-tiered waterfall plunging over dark rocks, framed by dense green deciduous trees. A chain-link fence runs along the bottom foreground, separating the viewer from the water and rocks. The sky is partly cloudy with patches of blue, and the overall scene blends urban industrial architecture with a natural water feature and lush greenery.

details.description: A multi‑story historic red‑brick industrial building stands on a wooded slope beside a small river gorge with a two‑tier waterfall cascading over dark rocks. The building has gabled ends and rows of tall arched windows with light-colored frames, characteristic of late‑19th/early‑20th‑century mill or factory architecture. Lush deciduous trees surround the scene, and a chain‑link fence runs along a riverside path in the foreground under a bright, partly cloudy sky.

The building is set against a bright, partly cloudy sky. In the foreground and center-right, a lush cluster of green trees surrounds a cascading waterfall that pours over dark rock formations into a lower pool, with a chain-link fence visible along the bottom edge of the frame. details.description: A low-angle, vertical shot capturing a historic red brick industrial building situated directly beside a cascading waterfall. The building, identified as the Hjula Væveri (Hjula Weaving Mill), features distinctive crow-stepped gables, a tall chimney, and rows of arched and rectangular windows characteristic of 19th-century industrial architecture. It stands on a rocky embankment overlooking the Vøyenfallene waterfall, which rushes over dark rocks in the lower right. The scene is framed by lush green deciduous trees in the foreground and background. A chain-link fence runs along the bottom right foreground, suggesting a pedestrian viewing path. The sky above is bright with scattered white clouds.

details.description: A vertical photograph capturing a scenic urban landscape featuring a waterfall and a historic industrial building. In the foreground, a waterfall, identified as Møllafossen, cascades over dark, jagged rocks into the Akerselva river. The water is white and frothy from the fall. To the left, a large, multi-story red-brick building with a gabled roof and numerous windows rises above the tree line. The building exhibits late 19th-century industrial architecture with arched window frames and decorative brickwork. Dense green deciduous trees surround the waterfall and partially obscure the base of the building. The sky is filled with soft, white clouds against a pale blue background. A chain-link fence is visible in the lower right corner, indicating a public path or viewing area.
